report:
## INCIDENT SUMMARY
A failure has been detected in the customer microservice, which may have a cascading impact on other services. The GNN Blast Radius Predictions indicate a high failure probability for several dependent services.

## PREDICTED IMPACT
The frontend, redis-manual, route, and driver services are predicted to be affected with failure probabilities of 47.9%, 15.7%, 13.2%, and 10.2% respectively, due to their dependencies on the customer microservice. The severity of the impact is moderate to high, as these services are critical to the overall functionality of the system.

## IMMEDIATE ACTIONS
The on-call team should: (1) investigate the customer microservice logs to identify the root cause of the failure, (2) restart the customer microservice to attempt a quick recovery, and (3) monitor the dependent services for signs of failure or degradation.

## TEAMS TO NOTIFY
The frontend, redis, route, and driver teams should be notified of the potential impact on their services, as they may need to take corrective actions or prepare for potential failures.

## RISK ASSESSMENT
The overall system risk level is moderate, as the failure of the customer microservice has the potential to impact multiple critical services, but the mysql service remains unaffected, providing a potential fallback or mitigation option.
